name: NexCMS
tagline: Headless content management system built for developers
description: NexCMS is a headless CMS — a content management system where the editing interface is separated from the front end that displays the content. Developers connect their own frontend; editors manage content through a clean dashboard. No opinionated templating, no framework lock-in.
forWho: Developers who need a flexible content backend without being tied to a specific front-end framework. Teams that want editors to manage content without touching code.
story: Built as a learning project that turned into something useful. The goal was to understand how headless CMS architectures actually work by building one from scratch — content modeling, API design, role-based access, media handling. It's become a useful internal tool for some of my other projects.
details: Content types defined via JSON schema. REST + GraphQL API output. Role-based access control for editor, author, and admin roles. Media upload and management. Built to be self-hosted or deployed to any Node-compatible platform.
